recent_calls.summary: Shirley Williams called to request a plumbing service for an issue with vent pipes through the roof, as suggested by a roofer. She scheduled a morning appointment for any day next week and confirmed her address and contact details. The agent noted her request and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up soon.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing herself but no further conversation occurred before the user hung up.
The call was very brief and did not progress beyond initial greetings and some unclear number exchanges. The user hung up shortly after the call started.
The user called and asked to get a hold of Daniel but the conversation was very brief and ended abruptly. The agent introduced herself but the call ended without completing the request.

The user, Albie from American Home Shield, called to schedule an AC service appointment for next Monday morning as the AC was not working again after a previous repair. The agent confirmed the details, noted the user's interest in maintenance program information, and received positive feedback from the user. The call ended successfully with a promise that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up soon.

The user called and was quickly transferred to a live customer service representative for further assistance. The agent initiated a cold transfer to another number to connect the user with the appropriate help.
The user requested to speak with JZ, and the agent attempted to transfer the call to the appropriate person. The call was successfully transferred without further conversation.
The user called to approve a service charge, but the agent did not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

The user requested to reschedule an HVAC estimate from Monday afternoon to Tuesday at 10 AM due to a scheduling conflict. The agent confirmed the details, noted the service is under American Home Shield warranty, and offered information about home maintenance programs, which the user accepted. The call ended with the agent confirming a follow-up and the user declining to provide feedback.
The user, Liz, left a voicemail message providing a callback number for inquiries about their business line of credit. The agent did not have a live conversation with the user.

The user, Marcy Turner, called to inquire about the number of plumbers Budget currently has as part of cleaning up their CRM system. The agent informed her that they are a full-service plumbing company but did not have the exact number and offered to connect her with a live agent for more details. The call was then successfully transferred to another agent.
The user called back and the agent attempted to assist by asking for more details, but the call was quickly transferred to another number for further help.

Rhonda called to schedule a maintenance appointment for her cooling system under a home warranty at her residence on 2415 Menard St, St Louis. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for Thursday, May 14th at 12 PM. The call ended with the agent asking for feedback and Rhonda expressing thanks.
The user called to return a phone call and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful and the call ended shortly after.

The user scheduled an air conditioner cleaning and seasonal check for Wednesday, May 13, 2026, at 10 AM, along with plumbing work to replace an outside faucet which must be done before the AC service. The agent confirmed the booking and collected positive feedback from the user. The call ended successfully with all user requests addressed.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Fatuki, called to schedule a plumbing service for a leaking water heater at a tenant's residence. The agent booked an appointment for Monday at 8 AM and collected the tenant's contact information for access. The user declined to provide feedback but confirmed the booking and expressed urgency for the repair.

The user called to check the arrival time of the technician. The agent confirmed the user's identity and provided the appointment details, stating that technician Vernon is scheduled to arrive between 11 AM and 12 PM. The user had no further questions and ended the call.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called back in response to a message from Budget and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent acknowledged the callback and initiated a transfer to another representative.

The user called to cancel a routine cooling maintenance appointment scheduled for the eleventh at 9 AM due to financial reasons. The agent confirmed the user's details and informed them that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up to assist with the cancellation and provide information about home maintenance programs. The user agreed to receive information about the maintenance programs.

The user, Rose, called to cancel a cooling service scheduled for the incorrect property at 3317 Humphrey Street. The agent confirmed the details and assured that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user called Budget and provided their ZIP code 63367. They requested to speak to a salesperson, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent successfully.
The agent attempted to engage the user by asking for their service address ZIP code, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.

The user called to schedule a city plumbing inspection on Brookshire Drive and requested to speak with a representative. The agent attempted to gather more details but transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Amy Guppill, called to inquire about plumbing services and booked a service appointment for a possible leaky pipe or washer hookup pipe replacement. The appointment was scheduled for Monday, May 11th at 8 AM. The call concluded with the user declining to provide feedback.

Leata Price-Land called to schedule an air conditioning pressure check follow-up appointment after a previous incomplete check due to low temperatures. The agent booked the appointment for Wednesday, May 13th at 4 PM as requested. The user provided feedback rating the experience as average and confirmed no confusion or missing information during the call.
The user received a call from someone they believe was a repairman. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent who could assist further and successfully transferred the call.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for an air pressure check on their cooling system, mentioning a missed follow-up from April 3rd. Due to poor call quality, the user requested to hang up and call back, and the agent transferred the call to another live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with an operator, and the agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without further interaction.

Sonia from American Home Shield's service resolutions department called to request diagnosis details for a cooling service performed at 718 Oak. The agent collected all necessary information and confirmed that a licensed technician would follow up with the diagnosis as soon as possible. Sonia declined additional information about maintenance programs and chose not to provide feedback.

Wayne from American Home Shield called to inquire about the status of a work order for an air conditioning system on behalf of Jacqueline Turner. The agent confirmed the details and transferred the call to a live representative for further assistance.

The user called to schedule an HVAC inspection due to condensation issues damaging ceilings and walls. The agent collected all necessary information, confirmed the appointment details for May 27th between 9 AM and 3 PM, and the user declined information about maintenance programs. The user provided positive feedback and the call ended successfully.
The user called back after receiving a call from Amy and was connected to a live agent for further assistance. The call was transferred successfully to another agent.

The user called to inform about a scheduling issue where the technician arrived earlier than the expected window, which affected their plans. The agent acknowledged the concern and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to reschedule their standard AC cleaning appointment from Monday to any morning next week except Monday. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them to confirm the new appointment time. The user declined additional information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the ease of rescheduling.

The user called to inquire about a scheduled appointment that was supposed to occur at 8 AM but no one had arrived. The agent attempted to assist by transferring the call to a live agent who could better handle the issue.

Shelia Clark called to inquire about the timing of her service appointment. The agent confirmed the appointment details, informing her that the technician Vernon would arrive between 11 AM and 12 PM. The user had no further questions and ended the call politely.

The user called to cancel an appointment for new AC installation scheduled for the next morning. The agent confirmed the cancellation and informed the user that the team would follow up on the next business day to confirm. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.

Louise called Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing's after-hours assistant to discuss a home warranty request for an outside HVAC unit check. She provided details about potential wiring issues and preferred a morning appointment between 8 and 9 AM. The agent confirmed the information, advised her to check with her home warranty about any fees, and received positive feedback from Louise before ending the call.

The user called to inquire about service availability in ZIP code 30019, but the agent informed them that service is not offered in that area. The user declined to provide a different ZIP code, and the call ended courteously.

Nancy Gongaware called to report a leaking hot water heater at her home in Saint Peters and requested a service visit for 9 AM the next day. The agent confirmed her details, scheduled the request, and collected positive feedback on the service experience. The call concluded successfully with no further issues.
The call was very brief and did not progress beyond initial greetings. The user and agent exchanged a few words but no meaningful conversation or service request occurred.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing herself and asking how she could help, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user, Priscilla, called Budget's after-hours assistant and requested to speak to a human agent. The assistant explained that live agents are unavailable after hours but offered to relay the message for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended without further details provided by the user.

The user, Rose, called after hours to return a call from Nicole regarding an American Home Shield dispatch. The agent informed her that live transfers were not available but assured her that Nicole would call back on the next business day. The call ended with no urgent issues and a promise of a callback.

Laura Teesdale called to clarify a service request for her HVAC system, specifically the air conditioning which was not cooling properly. The agent confirmed her details and scheduled a regular service appointment as soon as possible. Laura was satisfied with the assistance and provided positive feedback.

Janine from American Home Shield's authorizations department called to request details about a leaking evaporator coil in the cooling system. The agent confirmed the dispatch number and callback information, and assured Janine that the request would be passed to a licensed technician for review. The call ended with Janine declining to provide feedback.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed the transfer successfully.
The user called and requested to speak with Jacob. The agent attempted to connect the user to the requested person and successfully transferred the call.
